name: Kiri Jewelry Inc
description_short: Kiri Jewelry Inc is a pawn shop located in Miami, FL that buys, sells, and loans against jewelry and precious metals during extended weekday hours.
description_long: Kiri Jewelry Inc operates as a traditional pawn shop in the Flagler Street area of Miami, Florida. The business is positioned in a neighborhood with significant foot traffic and serves the local community's immediate financial and jewelry needs. Based on available information, the company functions as a collateral-based lending operation typical of pawn shops, accepting jewelry and precious metals as security for loans. The business maintains consistent operating hours throughout the week, opening at 9:30 AM on weekdays and 9:00 AM on Saturdays, closing at 5:30 PM and 5:00 PM respectively, indicating a retail-focused model serving daytime customers. Kiri Jewelry Inc distinguishes itself through its focus on jewelry and precious metals specifically, rather than general merchandise pawning. The company is reachable by phone at (305) 264-3621 for inquiries regarding loan terms, appraisals, or purchase offers on jewelry items. The primary caveat is that pawn loans involve surrendering personal items as collateral, and failure to repay results in loss of the item—making this a high-cost short-term financing option compared to traditional lending. Customers should understand that pawn loans typically carry interest rates significantly higher than personal loans or credit alternatives, though they offer faster access to cash without credit checks.
pros: Located at accessible address on W Flagler St in Miami with established phone line (305) 264-3621
Consistent 5-day-per-week operating schedule (Mon-Fri 9:30 AM-5:30 PM) provides reliable access
Saturday hours (9:00 AM-5:00 PM) offer weekend service option
Specializes in jewelry and precious metals, suggesting expertise in item valuation
No credit check required for pawn loans—accessible to those with poor or no credit history
Immediate cash access without application delays typical of traditional lending
Physical brick-and-mortar location allows in-person item evaluation and negotiation
cons: Website is a Waze directions page with minimal company information—no details about loan terms, interest rates, or fees
Pawn loans require surrendering personal items as collateral, risking permanent loss if unable to repay
Pawn interest rates typically 15-240% APR depending on state regulations, significantly higher than alternative lending
Limited online presence suggests minimal customer service or dispute resolution transparency
No information available regarding redemption periods, renewal policies, or item storage conditions
best_for: Individuals needing immediate cash ($100-$5,000 range) and willing to pledge jewelry or watches
People with poor credit or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
Those seeking short-term bridge financing while waiting for paychecks or other income
diagnosis: Kiri Jewelry Inc is best for consumers who need immediate cash and have jewelry or precious metals to pledge as collateral, especially those unable to qualify for traditional loans due to poor credit. The primary caveat is that pawn loans carry very high interest rates and risk permanent loss of personal items—this should only be considered when traditional lending options are unavailable and the borrower is confident in their ability to repay within the redemption period.
